name: Moriscos, Aldearrubia, and Aldealengua Loop
name_original: Aldearrubia - Las Canteras – circular desde Moriscos
description: An easy 15.0-mile mountain biking route near Moriscos, offering picturesque views of the countryside, reservoir, and Salamanca.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Moriscos, located in the Salamanca region of Spain, offer diverse terrain for outdoor enthusiasts. The landscape is characterized by picturesque countryside views, a reservoir, and distant vistas of the city of Salamanca. This area provides a mix of routes, from easy paths suitable for any fitness level to more challenging trails that require good fitness and advanced riding skills. The varied terrain ensures options for different mountain biking preferences. Best mountain bike trails around Moriscos The most popular mountain bike trail is Moriscos, Aldearrubia, and Aldealengua Loop, an easy 15.0 miles (24.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic views of the local countryside. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Pier – Roman Bridge (Salamanca) loop from Aldealengua, an easy 19.0 miles (30.5 km) path. This trail provides a pleasant ride with views towards Salamanca. Local mountain bikers also love the Mountainbike loop from Aldealengua, an 18.3 miles (29.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes. Mountain biking around Moriscos is defined by open countryside, reservoir views, and distant cityscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to moderate challenges. The routes in Moriscos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 240 reviews. More than 2000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Moriscos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of mountain bike trails can I find around Moriscos?
A: The Moriscos area offers a diverse range of mountain bike trails, from easy paths suitable for any fitness level to more challenging routes requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. The landscape features picturesque countryside views, a reservoir, and distant vistas of the city of Salamanca.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Moriscos?
A: Yes, Moriscos has several easy mountain bike trails perfect for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31442166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pier – Roman Bridge (Salamanca) loop from Aldealengua&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 19.0-mile (30.5 km) path offering a pleasant ride with views towards Salamanca.

Q: What is the typical length of mountain bike trails in the Moriscos area?
A: Mountain bike trails around Moriscos vary in length. Many popular routes range from approximately 15 miles (24 km) to over 30 miles (50 km). For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31444758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pond – Church of Aldearrubia loop from Aldealengua&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 31.2-mile (50.2 km) trail.

Q: What scenic views or landmarks can I expect to see while mountain biking in Moriscos?
A: While mountain biking in Moriscos, you can enjoy open countryside, reservoir views, and distant cityscapes of Salamanca. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31442166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pier – Roman Bridge (Salamanca) loop from Aldealengua&lt;/a&gt;, offer direct views towards the city. You might also consider visiting landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1392032" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Salamanca&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3759306" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor of Salamanca&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible nearby.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Moriscos?
A: The mountain bike trails in Moriscos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 240 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the scenic countryside views, and the variety of routes available for different skill levels.

Q: Is there convenient parking available near the mountain bike trails in Moriscos?
A: Yes, many trailheads and starting points for mountain bike routes in the Moriscos area, including those near Aldealengua and Cabrerizos, offer convenient parking options. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for precise parking locations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Moriscos?
A: The mild climate in the region, particularly around Motril and the broader area, allows for year-round outdoor activities. However, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking in Moriscos, avoiding the peak heat of summer and potential winter chill.

Q: Are there any moderate mountain bike trails for intermediate riders?
A: Absolutely. The Moriscos area has a significant number of moderate trails. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31705282" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountainbike loop from Aldealengua&lt;/a&gt; is an 18.3-mile (29.5 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 28 minutes, making it a great option for intermediate riders.

Q: Do I need any special permits to mountain bike in Moriscos?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for mountain biking on the established trails around Moriscos. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations and private property signs, especially when riding through agricultural areas.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Moriscos?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes in Moriscos are designed as loops, offering circular journeys. This allows riders to start and finish at the same point without needing to retrace their steps. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pond – Pitiegua Church loop from Cabrerizos&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 27.5-mile (44.3 km) route.

Q: Are the mountain bike trails around Moriscos dog-friendly?
A: While many trails in natural areas are generally accessible, specific rules regarding dogs can vary. It's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check for any local signage regarding pet access on individual routes.
tips:
The Cuesta de los Caenes section is part of a very accessible circular route near Cabrerizos, on the outskirts of Salamanca.
No shade, ideal for cool days.
Suitable for families and children.
Beautiful views of Cabrerizos, Salamanca in the distance, and the Tormes Valley.
The route passes by the Arroyo de Las Caenes stream, and the Cuesta de los Caenes is one of its most recognizable sections.
Beautiful views from the top of the hill towards the countryside and the Aldearrubia reservoir. The ascent is not difficult and you can opt for a more entertaining trialera for the descent.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Aldearrubia - Las Canteras – circular desde Moriscos trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending near Moriscos. While specific parking lots aren't detailed, you can typically find parking in or around the village of Moriscos to begin your ride.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail is generally accessible, there's no specific information regarding dog restrictions. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the mix of unpaved tracks and natural paths, and to be mindful of other riders and local wildlife.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: Given that the trail generally lacks shade, it's most enjoyable on cooler days. Spring and autumn are ideal, offering pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Early mornings in summer could also work, but avoid the midday heat.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access the Aldearrubia - Las Canteras – circular desde Moriscos mountain bike trail. It's freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is the Aldearrubia - Las Canteras – circular desde Moriscos trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is classified as 'easy' and is very suitable for beginners and all skill levels. It corresponds to STS S0 - S1 difficulty ratings, meaning it's non-technical and generally smooth. The ascent to Las Canteras is also not difficult.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features unpaved tracks (17.3 km) and gravel paths (2.97 km), including the very accessible Cuesta de los Caenes Gravel Track. There are also smaller sections of paved roads, asphalt, singletrack, and natural paths, offering a varied but generally smooth ride.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or views along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy picturesque views of the countryside, the Aldearrubia reservoir, and panoramic vistas of Cabrerizos and Salamanca in the distance. Key highlights include the Church of Aldearrubia and the beautiful Views from Las Canteras, which overlook the surrounding landscape and reservoir.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for families with children?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! This trail's 'easy' difficulty rating and accessible terrain make it an excellent choice for families and children looking for a pleasant mountain biking experience near Salamanca.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 24.1 kilometers (15.0 miles) long and typically takes about 1 hour and 55 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Camino de Mula, Camino de Aldearrubia, and the Cañada de Salamanca a Medina del Campo, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any shaded sections on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail generally lacks shade, so it's advisable to plan your ride for cooler parts of the day or bring appropriate sun protection, especially during warmer months.

waypoints.name: Cuesta de los Caenes Gravel Track
